picks.reasoning: Wrexham are sixth in the Championship for a reason — four wins from their last five, including a clean 2-0 at Swansea four days ago. Watford, meanwhile, are in absolute freefall: one win in five, just hammered 3-1 by Stoke, and missing several bodies through injury. Vicarage Road is not the fortress it was when Watford were flying, and Ladbrokes are charging a 2.20 premium on a home side that's collected 11 points from a possible 36. The form disparity here is glaring.

Arsenal's 1-28 to win a level tie (1-1 agg) without Odegaard, Timber, AND Merino is some serious market arrogance. The midfield creator is gone, the defensive cover is gone, and Leverkusen come in motivated after their set-piece coach got chirped at mid-game. Bayer have Terrier and Ben Seghir returning from injury and will not just roll over at the Emirates. A draw sending this to extra time at 5.75 looks significantly underpriced.

Valverde put his hat-trick in Madrid's scrapbook last week, and Los Blancos arrive at the Etihad with a 3-0 lead and Mbappe back from injury lurking on the bench. City need to score three without reply against arguably the best side in Europe, which means Pep sends everyone forward and leaves space a blind man could exploit. Madrid to win the 90-minute match at 6.0 is the kind of price that only exists because the crowd at the Etihad are going to make a lot of noise early on.
